article_title: Scientists Revive Dead Bacteria With Synthetic Genome Transplants, Creating the First Living Cells Built From Non-Living Parts

content_preview.title: Scientists Revive Dead Bacteria With Synthetic Genome Transplants, Creating the First Living Cells Built From Non-Living Parts
content_preview.summary: Researchers at the J. Craig Venter Institute transplanted synthetic genomes into chemically killed bacteria, producing 'zombie cells' that resumed normal growth and division.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Researchers at the J. Craig Venter Institute (JCVI) in La Jolla, California, have created what they call "zombie cells" — dead bacteria brought back to life by transplanting a complete synthetic genome into them. The technique, described in a [preprint posted to bioRxiv on March 13](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.64898/2026.03.13.711674v1), represents the first time scientists have constructed living, synthetic bacterial cells from non-living parts, and it could open new pathway...
